channel.title: Seattle Center Events
channel.description: Upcoming events for Seattle Center Events

item.title: Seattle Center Sculpture Walk 2026
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Seattle Center

Experience temporary art installations on our grounds throughout the year.

Event details

item.title: Seattle Center Sculpture Walk 2026
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Seattle Center

Experience temporary art installations on our grounds throughout the year.

Event details

item.title: Shen Yun 2026
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Marion Oliver McCaw Hall

The gong resounds, the curtain opens, and fairies emerge from a sea of billowing clouds. Mongolians ride on horseback across grasslands as vast as the sky. Dynasties rise and fall—as ancient legends of love, loss, humor and valor unfold before your eyes.

Event details

item.title: Mary Jane
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Seattle Repertory Theatre

Poignantly humorous and deeply cathartic, this semi-autobiographical drama by Tony-nominated playwright Amy Herzog is a love letter to caregivers and support systems of all kinds.

Event details

item.title: Seattle Center Sculpture Walk 2026
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Seattle Center

Experience temporary art installations on our grounds throughout the year.

Event details

item.title: Shen Yun 2026
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Marion Oliver McCaw Hall

The gong resounds, the curtain opens, and fairies emerge from a sea of billowing clouds. Mongolians ride on horseback across grasslands as vast as the sky. Dynasties rise and fall—as ancient legends of love, loss, humor and valor unfold before your eyes.

Event details

item.title: Seattle Kraken vs Utah Mammoth
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Climate Pledge Arena

The Seattle Kraken host the Utah Mammoth at Climate Pledge Arena.

Event details

item.title: Mary Jane: Open Captioning
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Seattle Repertory Theatre

Poignantly humorous and deeply cathartic, this semi-autobiographical drama by Tony-nominated playwright Amy Herzog is a love letter to caregivers and support systems of all kinds.

item.title: Seattle Center Sculpture Walk 2026
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Seattle Center

Experience temporary art installations on our grounds throughout the year.

Event details

item.title: Robin Hood
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Children's Theatre

A wannabe king, lawless law enforcement, and the rich hoarding all the wealth—it’s dark times in Nottingham. But out of the mists of nearby Sherwood Forest comes the hero we’ve been waiting for.

Event details

item.title: Shen Yun 2026
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 7 PM

The gong resounds, the curtain opens, and fairies emerge from a sea of billowing clouds. Mongolians ride on horseback across grasslands as vast as the sky. Dynasties rise and fall—as ancient legends of love, loss, humor and valor unfold before your eyes.

Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Fisher Pavilion

Where kids shine, brands discover and dreams begin.

item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 5 PM

item.title: Kraken Pregame in the Armory 25/26
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Armory

Pregame at the Armory, presented by WaFd Bank, is your go-to spot for game day vibes before every Kraken weekend home game at Climate Pledge Arena. Two hours before puck drop, the Armory comes alive with Kraken energy.

Event details

item.description:

item.title: Appropriate: Preview Performance
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Seattle Repertory Theatre

When the Lafayettes descend upon their late father’s crumbling plantation home for an estate sale, they unearth an appalling secret that pits them against one another.

Event details

item.title: Mary Jane
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Seattle Repertory Theatre

Poignantly humorous and deeply cathartic, this semi-autobiographical drama by Tony-nominated playwright Amy Herzog is a love letter to caregivers and support systems of all kinds.

item.title: Seattle Center Sculpture Walk 2026
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Seattle Center

Event details

item.title: Seattle Cherry Blossom & Japanese Cultural Festival
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Armory

Seattle Center Festál presents Seattle Cherry Blossom & Japanese Cultural Festival in partnership with Seattle Cherry Blossom & Japanese Cultural Festival Committee (SCBJCF). The festival deepens the understanding and serves as a vehicle for sharing Japanese and Japanese American culture and heritage through hundreds of performing and martial arts, exhibits and demonstrations, hands on kids’ activities, culinary treats, and more!

Event details

item.title: Robin Hood
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Children's Theatre

A wannabe king, lawless law enforcement, and the rich hoarding all the wealth—it’s dark times in Nottingham. But out of the mists of nearby Sherwood Forest comes the hero we’ve been waiting for.

Event details

item.title: Appropriate: Preview Performance
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Seattle Repertory Theatre

When the Lafayettes descend upon their late father’s crumbling plantation home for an estate sale, they unearth an appalling secret that pits them against one another.

Event details

item.title: Giselle
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Marion Oliver McCaw Hall

A mountain village in a lush forest becomes the backdrop to love, betrayal, and otherworldly specters in this compelling masterpiece.

Event details

item.title: Mary Jane
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Poignantly humorous and deeply cathartic, this semi-autobiographical drama by Tony-nominated playwright Amy Herzog is a love letter to caregivers and support systems of all kinds.

item.title: An Evening with Gabor Mate: The Myth of Normal
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Marion Oliver McCaw Hall

Embark on a profound journey into the intricate landscapes of the human mind and soul with renowned physician, speaker, and bestselling author, Dr. Gabor Maté.

Event details
